title: cytisus nigricans subsp. nigricans
intro: Cytisus nigricans subsp. nigricans, also known as Black Broom, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its dark brown or black pods and yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Cytisus nigricans subsp. nigricans include its potential to help with digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
